title: fleet GitHub repository settings posture
description: Fleet-canonical desired posture for GitHub repository governance settings that aren't covered by lint-github-settings.mts (which owns the PATCH /repos/{owner}/{repo} surface: merge policy, features, branch protection). This file owns the governance surfaces reachable only via separate endpoints or only via the Settings UI — interaction limits and pull-request limits. The conform check reads this posture, compares each fleet repo's live settings, and re-asserts via `gh api` where a REST endpoint exists.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for editor autocompletion. Conventionally `./github-settings.schema.json` — config and schema live side-by-side in `.config/fleet/`.

description.description: Human-readable note about what this posture declares. Read by humans, not parsed.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes; readers gate on it.

interactionLimit.description: Temporary interaction restriction posture (Settings → Moderation → Interaction limits). GitHub interaction limits are EXPIRY-BOUND — they auto-lapse after the chosen window. This declares the desired STANDING posture; the conform check re-asserts it (resetting the expiry clock) whenever the live limit is absent, lapsed, or weaker than declared. NOTE: interaction limits cannot be set on PRIVATE repositories (GitHub returns 405); the check skips private repos with a manual-verification note. Omit this block to declare 'no interaction limit desired'.

limit.description: Which accounts are temporarily blocked from interacting. `existing_users` blocks brand-new accounts; `contributors_only` limits to prior contributors; `collaborators_only` limits to repository collaborators. Maps to the `limit` field of PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/interaction-limits.

expiry.description: How long the interaction limit stays active before GitHub auto-lifts it. Maps to the `expiry` field of PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/interaction-limits. The conform check re-asserts to reset this window when the live limit has lapsed.

pullRequestLimit.description: Pull-request limit posture (Settings → General → Pull Requests → 'Limit open pull requests from users without write access' + 'Maximum open pull requests per user'). As of this writing GitHub exposes NO stable REST endpoint for these fields — they are not present on GET /repos/{owner}/{repo} and have no dedicated endpoint. This block therefore captures INTENT only: the conform check reports the desired values and emits a manual-verification note pointing at the Settings UI; it never PUTs them. Re-probe `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}` for a future field before wiring enforcement.

maxOpenPerUser.description: Maximum simultaneous open pull requests per user (GitHub UI range 1–1000, default 5). Intent-only — no REST endpoint to enforce.

restrictToWriteAccess.description: Whether to limit open pull requests from users without write access. Intent-only — no REST endpoint to enforce.
